title: Gently Smoky Malaysian Chicken Curry
description: A smoky and creamy Malaysian chicken curry featuring dry white wine and papillote, perfect for a weeknight dinner
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together curry powder, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Add the chicken to the bowl and toss to coat with the spice mixture.
In a large saucepan, combine the white wine and coconut milk, and bring to a simmer over medium heat.
Add the chicken to the saucepan and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the chopped cilantro.
Cut a piece of parchment paper into a large square and place the chicken and sauce in the center of the paper.
Fold the parchment paper over the chicken to create a papillote and bake for 25 minutes.
Remove the papillote from the oven and let it rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
